data.title: Castellum AB (publ) reports FY results
data.description:
• Castellum AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:CWQXF): FY Net operating income totalled SEK 6,786 M 
  • Income totalled SEK 9,849 M (9,806), of which SEK 65 M (0) pertained to estimated indemnity.
  • Net investments totalled SEK -634 M (-1,460), of which SEK 2,502 M (3,410) pertained to new construction, extensions and reconstructions, SEK 67 M (336) to acquisitions and SEK -3,203 M (-5,206) to sales.

data.title: Sanofi to sell 50% stake in consumer health unit to CD&R
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) has entered a share purchase agreement in relation to the sale of a 50% controlling stake in Opella, its consumer healthcare business, to private equity firm CD&R.

The terms of the transaction remain unchanged from those previously disclosed, and closing is expected to take place in the second of 2025 at the earliest.

Bpifrance is expected to participate as a minority shareholder with a c.2% stake in Opella, with Sanofi remaining a significant shareholder.

Sanofi first entered into negotiations with CD&R for the potential sale in October 2024. Bloomberg, at the time, had reported the deal value to be around EUR 15B, or $16.4B.

Headquartered in France, Opella sells over-the-counter and vitamins, minerals, and supplements, with a portfolio of 100 brands including Allegra, Doliprane, Novanight, Icy Hot, and Dulcolax. It manages 13 manufacturing sites and four research and innovation centers.

data.title: Google to pay €326M to settle tax case in Italy - report
data.description:
Milan's prosecutors said they had requested to drop a case against the European unit of Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google after the company agreed to pay €326M (about $340M) to settle a tax claim, Reuters reported.

The settlement spans the period between 2015 and 2019 and covers sanctions, penalties and interest, the report added, citing a statement from prosecutors.

Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

In June 2024, Italy requested Google to pay €1B (around $1.07B) in unpaid taxes and penalties, the report noted.

The Milan prosecutors alleged that Google did not submit and pay taxes on revenue generated in Italy, based on claims of the digital infrastructure Google has in the European country, according to the report.

In 2017, Google paid €306M euros to settle a previous case which found that the company had a permanent presence in Italy, the report added.

data.title: Ontex GAAP EPS of €0.25, revenue of €1.86B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Ontex press release (OTCPK:ONXXF): FY GAAP EPS of €0.25.
  • Revenue of €1.86B (+3.9% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA was €223 million, up 28%. Sustained delivery of the cost transformation program added €70 million, driving competitiveness and profitability.
  • Free cash flow amounted to €48 million, a significant increase compared to €9 million in 2023.
  • Capital expenditure rose to €(112) million, reaching close to 6% of revenue in Core Markets, and €(39) million one-time spent was related mostly to the transformation of the Belgian operations.
  • 2025 outlook Based on solid delivery in the last two years, Ontex is well positioned to successfully complete its intensive three-year transformation journey, and thereby expects the following for 2025: > Revenue to grow by 3% to 5% like for like, supported by double-digit volume growth in North America; > Adjusted EBITDA to grow by 4% to 7%, supported by revenue growth and further improvement of operational efficiencies; > Free cash flow to remain strong, while continuing the step-up of investments in Ontex’s transformation. This stepup will be nearing completion by the end of 2025.

data.title: France to host second summit on Ukraine; U.S.-Russia talks signal diplomatic reset
data.description:
France is expected to host a second summit on the Ukraine war and European security on Wednesday, diplomatic sources told Reuters, as the U.S. and Russia continue negotiations after an initial meeting that excluded Kyiv.

For the second summit, French President Emmanuel Macron invited European countries that did not attend the emergency Paris meeting held earlier this week - Norway, Finland, Belgium, Sweden, Greece, Lithuania, Estonia, Czech Republic, Latvia, and Romania. NATO ally Canada was also invited for Wednesday's meeting.

The emergency Paris meeting on Monday, which included leaders from Britain, Germany and NATO, failed to reach a deal on security guarantees for Ukraine and defense spending.

U.S.-Russia talks

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov on Tuesday in Saudi Arabia. The meeting excluded Ukraine and European allies, and went beyond discussions over Kyiv - signaling a major diplomatic reset by Washington.

The U.S. and Russia agreed to hold more talks, and appoint high-level teams to work towards ending the Ukraine war in a way that's acceptable to all sides. They will also lay the groundwork for future cooperation on matters of mutual geopolitical interest and economic and investment opportunities, according to the U.S. State Department.

Donald Trump said he will probably meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin this month. "I think I have the power to end this war," the U.S. president said at a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago resort.

Trump also addressed concerns of European countries and Kyiv over not being invited to the negotiations in Riyadh. "Well, you've been there for three years. You should have ended it…," Trump said at a news conference at his Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Florida. "You should have never started it."

To note, during Tuesday's negotiations, Russia demanded that Ukraine should not be allowed to join NATO, and opposed the idea of NATO troops being deployed to enforce any potential peace deal, Reuters reported.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Fiverr International beats Q1, FY25 revenue outlook
European markets mixed as traders assess earnings, potential tariffs
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.34%

UK's inflation rises to 3% in January

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.39%

France (CAC:IND) -0.14%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) was little changed on Wednesday as markets assessed U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff talks, a host of earnings releases, and a hotter-than-expected U.K. inflation print.

Sectors and bourses were trading in mixed territory, after the benchmark's record close in the previous session.

On the corporate front, BAE Systems' (OTCPK:BAESF) shares slipped despite the British defense giant reporting a record order book, while Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) was trading lower after posting a year-on-year decline in adjusted earnings in its preliminary annual results.

Europe’s largest lender HSBC (HSBC) meanwhile reported an annual pre-tax profit of $32.31 billion on Wednesday and initiated a share buy-back of up to $2 billion.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points 4.56%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points at 2.52%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points 4.60%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: BAE Systems Non-GAAP EPS of 68.50P, revenue of £28.34B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• BAE Systems plc press release (OTCPK:BAESY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 68.50P.
  • Revenue of £28.34B (+14.0% Y/Y).
  • Free cash flow was £2,505M, reflecting a high level of customer advances towards the end of the year and strong operational cash conversion.
  • Order backlog grew by 11% to a record £77.8B, which included order backlog of £3.0B related to SMS.

data.title: Glencore GAAP EPS of -$0.13, revenue of $230.94B misses by $550M
data.description:
• Glencore press release (OTCPK:GLCNF): FY GAAP EPS of -$0.13.
  • Revenue of $230.94B (+6.0% Y/Y) misses by $550M.
  • $14.4 billion Adjusted EBITDA, down 16%, primarily reflecting weaker energy coal prices.
  • Funds from operations of $10.5 billion, up 11%.
  • Net cash purchase and sale of PP&E: $6.7 billion compared to $5.6 billion in 2023; EVR accounts for majority of the increase.
  • Adjusted EBITDA mining margins were 28% in our metals operations, 45% in steelmaking coal and 32% in energy coal.

data.title: Nexans S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Nexans S.A. (OTCPK:NXPRF): FY Net income at €283 million, up +27% versus 2023
  • Revenue of €7.1B (+8.7% Y/Y)
  • Full-year 2025 guidance announced o Adjusted EBITDA of between €770 and 850 million
  • Free Cash Flow of between €225 and 325 million
  • Net debt at €681 million and 0.85x leverage ratio
